invariants.statement: A host OS update (kernel, apt, unattended-upgrades, snap, desktop) never alters, corrupts, or interrupts LifeOS durable state.
invariants.predicate: The LifeOS durable tier is byte-identical before and after a host apt full-upgrade plus reboot.

invariants.statement: A LifeOS update never modifies the host kernel, host packages, /etc, or the host user environment.
invariants.predicate: The host manifest (dpkg selections, /etc content hash, kernel version, login shell) is identical before and after a LifeOS update.

invariants.statement: LifeOS runs as native host processes inside a Nix-declared user-namespace envelope; no hypervisor and no container daemon sits in the hot path.
invariants.predicate: The envelope process tree contains no qemu, kvm, dockerd, or containerd ancestor; the envelope is entered via bwrap as a direct child of the session supervisor.

invariants.statement: In-envelope runtime latency equals bare-native latency; there is no hypervisor tax.
invariants.predicate: The in-envelope benchmark suite completes within 2 percent of the bare-native baseline on the same host.

invariants.statement: Every byte of LifeOS state — PostgreSQL/RuVector macro-state, redb transient projections, agent/session/tool state, secrets, raw ingested bytes — lives on LifeOS-owned durable storage.
invariants.predicate: Every enumerated state root in the tier map resolves to a durable-tier path, and a kill-plus-reboot cycle reproduces the durable tier byte-identically.

invariants.statement: Nothing durable lives on the host /run tmpfs.
invariants.predicate: The tier map contains zero durable entries whose target path starts with /run/, and the CI guard fails when any durable env var points at /run.

invariants.statement: The volatile tier (caches, cargo-target, build tmp, rustup toolchains) stays ephemeral on tmpfs and is never promoted to durable storage.
invariants.predicate: Every cache-class entry in the tier map is classified volatile with a tmpfs current path, and no durable-tier mount hosts cache-class data.

invariants.statement: No home-owned residual acts as an active owner; every LifeOS binary, runtime path, and active config resolves solely through the single Nix profile frontdoor.
invariants.predicate: A full sweep finds zero active owners under ~/.claude, ~/.local/share/<tool>, or ~/FlexNetOS, and the CI guard fails on any home-owned active owner.

invariants.statement: The shipped LifeOS release is fully self-contained: it runs on a clean host with no Nix daemon and no shared /nix/store.
invariants.predicate: The clean-host boot test passes, and the RPATH/ldd audit of the released closure shows no unresolved /nix/store references outside the shipped bundle.

invariants.statement: After any host reboot the LifeOS envelope re-materializes and re-attaches to its durable state automatically or via a single deliberate command.
invariants.predicate: The cold-reboot harness restores PostgreSQL/RuVector, redb, the Glass/Engine front door, and resumable session context without manual repair.

invariants.statement: Host resources are acquired only through the declared control plane, and every acquisition records the prior host state for restore.
invariants.predicate: Every acquire action has a matching audit record containing a restore snapshot taken before control was taken.

invariants.statement: Every acquisition has a clean release that restores the recorded prior host state; controls are reversible and time-bounded, never a permanent takeover.
invariants.predicate: An acquire/release cycle across every registered resource leaves the observable host state equal to its pre-acquire snapshot.

invariants.statement: The little brother always functions: during and after any LifeOS control action the host OS can update, reboot, and run its own daemons normally.
invariants.predicate: Host update and reboot performed while LifeOS holds and then releases resources complete normally with no host-side degradation.

invariants.statement: Retained services (Omada controller and peers) run Nix-supervised inside the envelope, not under ad-hoc host docker or KVM.
invariants.predicate: Omada serves under Nix supervision while docker.service, containerd.service, libvirtd, and qemu-kvm boot units are disabled, and the host still functions normally.

invariants.statement: meta/var is never bind-mounted into any container runtime.
invariants.predicate: The container-mount assertion scan finds zero meta/var paths in any docker/containerd mount table.

invariants.statement: Host update mechanisms (unattended-upgrades, kernel installs, snap refreshes) are gated or observed by the LifeOS control plane during active work, and reboots are deliberate; the host stays free to update normally otherwise.
invariants.predicate: With a live session, kernel/snapd/accountsservice upgrades are held and auto-reboot is disabled; with no session, the host updates and reboots unassisted (the drdave path).

invariants.statement: The portable release carries its own store closure; /nix/store is a development-time dependency only.
invariants.predicate: The release artifact bundles or relocates its complete closure, and deleting the host /nix/store does not affect the running release.
